steps.title: Pinpoint the key explanation
steps.content: Valli and Brenner concluded that sediment influx was “spatially diffuse” — sources distributed throughout the stream system rather than concentrated at one primary location.

steps.title: Find supporting evidence
steps.content:
The correct answer directly supports this: bank collapses at multiple upstream points create distributed sediment sources entering throughout the reach, preventing the typical grain-size sorting that develops from a concentrated source.

The other choices either assert a single primary source, address sediment type rather than source distribution, or place tributary input downstream of the sampling sites.

That is the correct answer.
